OIML Organisation Internationale de Métrologie Légale

RésolutionsRéunionsÀ propos

À propos de cette archive

Un registre numérique des résolutions des réunions du CIML et des Conférences OIML, de 2004 à aujourd'hui.

Types d'actions

Chaque résolution est composée d'actions typées qui catégorisent la décision du comité (par exemple, demander une action, approuver un document ou remercier un hôte). Ce typage sémantique permet un filtrage et une analyse avancés des activités historiques du comité.

DemandeRemercieNommeApprouveRésoutDirectsAsksEncouragesAcceptsChargeNominatesDécideAgreesAdoptsÉtablitAccueilleCreatesRecommendsSoutientNoteRecognizesConfirmeAppreciationAllocatesSoutientDisbandsPrend acteAssignsAppreciates

À propos de l'OIML

Organisation Internationale de Métrologie Légale

La métrologie légale — la pratique et la science de la mesure qui touche au commerce, à la santé, à la sécurité et à l'environnement.

1955Fondée en
63États membres
66Membres correspondants
Site officielÉtats membresGitHubLinkedIn
Informations techniques

Format Edoxen

Resolutions in this archive are stored in plain-text YAML files using the Edoxen format. This structured representation ensures resolutions remain human-readable while being entirely machine-parsable.

metadata:
  title: Resolutions of the 17th OIML Conference, Paris, France
  dates:
  - start: '2024-10-18'
    end: '2024-10-25'
    kind: meeting
  source: OIML Conference Secretariat (BIML)
  venue: Paris, France

resolutions:
- identifier: Conference/2025/01
  subject: OIML Conference
  title: Approves the agenda for the 17th International Conference on Legal Metrology
  dates:
  - start: '2024-10-15'
    kind: decision
  actions:
  - type: approves
    message: |
      Approves the agenda for the 17th International Conference
      on Legal Metrology (OIML Conference).
  considerations:
  - type: noting
    message: |
      Approves the agenda for the 17th International Conference...
  approvals:
  - degree: unanimous
    message: Approved without objection

Each file contains two main sections: metadata, containing information about the meeting, and a resolutions array, detailing the individual decisions made. A resolution includes its identifier, subject, title, relevant dates, any context under considerations, and the actions mandated by the committee.

View the Edoxen schema on GitHub →

Cycle de vie d'une résolution

Each resolution in the Edoxen model follows a structured lifecycle, captured through three interconnected sections:

1

Considerations

The context and background that led to the resolution. Each consideration has a type (e.g., noting, recalling, recognising) and a message explaining what the committee observed or referenced.

2

Actions

The decisions themselves — what the committee resolved to do. Each action carries a semantic type (e.g., requests, approves, appoints) that categorizes the nature of the decision, along with the detailed message.

3

Approvals

How the resolution was formally adopted, including the degree of consensus (e.g., unanimous, consensus) and any relevant notes about the approval process.

A single resolution may contain multiple considerations, actions, and approvals — together forming a complete record of the committee's decision-making process.

Schéma d'URN

Every resolution is assigned a Uniform Resource Name following the OIML URN specification, format urn:oiml:doc:<body>:resolution:<id>.

  • CIML resolutions:urn:oiml:doc:ciml:resolution:<year>-<seq>
    Example: urn:oiml:doc:ciml:resolution:2025-44
  • Conference resolutions:urn:oiml:doc:conf:resolution:<session>.<seq>
    Example: urn:oiml:doc:conf:resolution:17.01

Schéma de DOI

Every resolution is registered with a DOI under the 10.63493/resolutions/ prefix, resolved via doi.org.

  • CIML:10.63493/resolutions/ciml<YYYY><NN>
    Example: 10.63493/resolutions/ciml202544 → doi.org/10.63493/resolutions/ciml202544
  • Conference:10.63493/resolutions/conf<YYYY><NN>
    Example: 10.63493/resolutions/conf202501 → doi.org/10.63493/resolutions/conf202501

<YYYY> is the four-digit meeting year; <NN> is the zero-padded per-meeting sequence.

OIML

Organisation Internationale de Métrologie Légale

La métrologie légale — la pratique et la science de la mesure qui touche au commerce, à la santé, à la sécurité et à l'environnement.
